dataset: Wikimedia Commons (Vermeer, Girl with a Pearl Earring)

pi_items.source: Task description states 'You are digitizing artwork for a museum's online archive'; this is standard digital archivist workflow for museum collections digitization
Task description references museum online archive; correcting photographed artworks is a core museum digitization workflow
Task description: 'Export as painting_corrected.png to the Desktop'; export_result.sh checks for /home/ga/Desktop/painting_corrected.png; verified in live test that path is writable

Task step 1: 'Use GIMP's Perspective tool to correct the distortion'; setup_task.sh applies perspective transform with find_coeffs() mapping a rectangle to trapezoid with corners at (100,60), (820,12), (816,613), (80,558). Left side height=498px, right side height=601px — 103px difference. Verified visually in live test screenshots (06_gimp_strong_perspective.png)

Task step 2: 'Crop tightly to just the painting, removing all visible wall area'; gallery photo is 1300x950 with painting centered at offset (236,131); wall is warm beige RGB(210,190,150) surrounding the painting on all sides

Task step 3: 'Scale the result to exactly 1200 pixels wide (maintain aspect ratio)'; ground truth expected_output_width=1200, expected_output_height=900 (from 800:600 = 4:3 ratio); verified in /tmp/perspective_painting_gt.json from live test

Task step 4: 'Correct the warm color cast using Colors > Curves'; setup_task.sh applies R+15, G+5, B-20 color shift. Live test measured gallery_photo.jpg channel means: R=163.0, G=144.0, B=108.8 (channel std=22.45). Original painting channel std=6.74. Agent must reduce red, increase blue to neutralize

pi_items.verified_value: Warm yellow-orange cast: R+15, G+5, B-20 applied globally. Gallery photo channel means: R=163.0, G=144.0, B=108.8 (std=22.45). To correct, agent should boost blue channel by ~20 and reduce red by ~15 in Curves
pi_items.source: Directly from setup_task.sh lines 165-168; confirmed in live test via Python image analysis (evidence 08_data_files_check.txt: 'Channels: R=162.9 G=144.0 B=108.6, Color cast std: 22.47')

pi_items.verified_value: 1200x900 pixels (1200 wide, 4:3 aspect ratio from 800x600 painting)
pi_items.source: Task description: 'Scale the result to exactly 1200 pixels wide (maintain aspect ratio)'. Original painting is 800x600 = 4:3. 1200 * (600/800) = 900. Confirmed in /tmp/perspective_painting_gt.json: expected_output_width=1200, expected_output_height=900

pi_items.source: Live test confirmed file exists (08_data_files_check.txt: 'painting_ground_truth.png: PNG image data, 800 x 600'). Channel means computed independently from setup script Python code

privileged_info_summary: The task requires extracting a painting from a gallery photograph (gallery_photo.jpg, 1300x950 JPEG on Desktop). The painting is Vermeer's 'Girl with a Pearl Earring' downloaded from Wikimedia Commons (resized to 800x600, 4:3 aspect ratio), surrounded by a 14px gold frame, and composited onto a warm beige wall (RGB ~210,190,150 after cast). The photograph has significant perspective distortion — the painting appears as a trapezoid with the left side 498px tall and right side 601px tall (103px difference), simulating a camera angle from the right. A warm yellow-orange color cast was applied: R+15, G+5, B-20, producing gallery photo channel means of R=163, G=144, B=109 (channel std=22.4, indicating strong imbalance). The agent must: (1) use GIMP's Perspective tool (Shift+P) to drag 4 corner handles to make the painting rectangular, (2) crop to remove the wall and frame, (3) scale to exactly 1200 pixels wide (expected output 1200x900), (4) correct the color cast using Colors > Curves by boosting blue ~20 and reducing red ~15 to achieve neutral balance (target channel std <7), and (5) export as painting_corrected.png to the Desktop. GIMP 2.10.30 is running with the image pre-loaded. The Perspective tool requires clicking and dragging handles — there is no numeric input field for perspective angles, making this intrinsically harder than rotation tasks. The painting trapezoid corners in the image are approximately at: TL=(336,191), TR=(1056,143), BR=(1052,744), BL=(316,689).
